industries.name: Fundamental Nuclear & Atomic Research
uses: Named after Tennessee, home of Oak Ridge National Laboratory — which made the discovery possible by supplying 22mg of berkelium-249 after 250 days of continuous reactor irradiation in the HFIR reactor. The most multinational superheavy element discovery, requiring unique US national laboratory capabilities. Tennessine is the second-heaviest halogen (Group 17) but predicted to behave as a metal rather than a nonmetal.

Longest-lived known isotope: 294Ts, t½ = 51 milliseconds. First synthesized: 2010, JINR Dubna / Oak Ridge National Laboratory / Vanderbilt University / University of Nevada Las Vegas — confirmed 2016.

Research probes the limits of nuclear stability, tests relativistic quantum chemistry predictions in superheavy atoms, and searches for the theoretical island of stability predicted around Z=114, N=184.
All production requires specialized particle accelerators and isotope reactor facilities — this element cannot be made by any other means.

companies.name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
companies.use: Made tennessine discovery possible by producing 22mg of berkelium-249 — the essential target material — after 250 days of continuous irradiation in the High Flux Isotope Reactor (HFIR), followed by rapid chemical separation and shipment to Dubna. Without ORNL's unique Bk-249 production capability, tennessine could not have been synthesized. The element is named after Tennessee in ORNL's honor.

companies.name: JINR Dubna
companies.use: Led the synthesis experiments, bombarding the ORNL-supplied berkelium-249 with calcium-48 ions at the Dubna Gas-Filled Recoil Separator. Oganessian's group detected tennessine atoms through their alpha decay chains and performed the nuclear property measurements establishing element 117.

companies.name: Vanderbilt University
companies.use: Key US academic partner in the tennessine discovery. Contributed detector development, nuclear theory support, and data analysis. Joseph Hamilton and Akunori Ramayya of Vanderbilt were co-authors on the discovery publication and part of the core collaboration team.

companies.name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
companies.use: Contributed nuclear theory calculations and decay chain analysis supporting tennessine's discovery claim. LLNL's long-standing collaboration with JINR on superheavy elements provided the scientific framework for interpreting the tennessine decay data.
